steps.title: Use the cone volume formula
steps.content: Use the given diameter to find the radius, then substitute the radius and height into the cone volume formula.

steps.title: Extract the radius and state the cone volume formula
steps.content: Given: height h = 21 cm and base diameter = 8 cm. The radius is half the diameter: r = 4 cm. Use the cone volume formula: $$V = \frac{1}{3}\pi r^2 h$$

steps.title: Substitute and simplify to find n
steps.content:
Substitute $r = 4$ and $h = 21$:
$$V = \frac{1}{3}\pi (4)^2 (21)$$
Simplify $(4)^2 = 16$:
$$V = \frac{1}{3}\pi \cdot 16 \cdot 21$$
Simplify $\frac{21}{3} = 7$:
$$V = 16 \cdot 7 \cdot \pi = 112\pi$$
Since the volume is expressed as $n\pi$ cm³, n = 112.
